source: OSWG consensus whitepapers; Henry et al. 1999, 2023; Frazier 2015; Levin et al. 2014
description: Expected class effects for phosphorothioate-backbone oligonucleotides (PS-ASOs, siRNAs). Hybridization-independent effects driven by PS backbone chemistry.

expected_findings.description: Renal proximal tubular basophilic granules - oligonucleotide accumulation
expected_findings.severity_threshold: Grade 4, or any grade with concurrent tubular degeneration/necrosis, elevated BUN/creatinine, or proteinuria
expected_findings.rationale: OSWG consensus: basophilic granules alone reflect oligonucleotide accumulation via megalin-mediated endocytosis; no functional impairment.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Grade 3, or any grade with concurrent hepatocellular degeneration/necrosis or ALT/AST >5x baseline
expected_findings.rationale: Isolated Kupffer cell changes represent drug accumulation without hepatocellular toxicity.

expected_findings.description: Splenic lymphoid hyperplasia / increased extramedullary hematopoiesis
expected_findings.severity_threshold: Grade 4, or spleen weight >2x control, or severe cytopenias
expected_findings.species_note: More pronounced in rodents due to CpG-TLR9 sensitivity. NHPs show milder changes.
expected_findings.rationale: Moderate lymphoid hyperplasia reflects immune stimulation - a known pharmacological consequence of PS backbone.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Grade 3, or any grade with tissue destruction
expected_findings.rationale: Macrophage uptake of PS-ASOs is a normal clearance mechanism.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Grade 3, or any grade with necrosis, extensive edema, or systemic inflammatory response
expected_findings.rationale: Local innate immune response to PS-ASO via TLR9 and complement activation at injection site.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Grade 2 - ALWAYS ADVERSE when present. Represents transition from accumulation to nephrotoxicity.
expected_findings.rationale: Even minimal tubular degeneration/necrosis represents functional impairment, unlike isolated basophilic granules.

expected_findings.species_note: More pronounced with first-gen PS-ASOs. Monkey is the most sensitive species.
expected_findings.rationale: PS backbone binds platelet factor 4 and other heparin-binding proteins.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: >2x baseline aPTT or accompanied by clinical bleeding
expected_findings.species_note: Most pronounced in NHPs. Monkey aPTT is the most sensitive coagulation endpoint for PS-ASOs.
expected_findings.rationale: PS-ASOs bind intrinsic pathway coagulation factors via aptameric interactions. The PS backbone mimics heparin's polyanionic structure.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: >5x baseline (2nd-gen) or >3x baseline (LNA/cEt high-affinity) with histopathological correlates
expected_findings.species_note: Rodent-predominant at standard doses. NHP and human ALT elevations rare with 2-MOE gapmers.
expected_findings.rationale: Two mechanisms: CpG/non-CpG TLR stimulation (rodent-specific, mild) and RNase H1-dependent hepatotoxicity (LNA, sequence-dependent, potentially severe).
Same mechanisms as ALT elevation. Parallels ALT pattern.

expected_findings.typical_magnitude: 1.0-1.3x baseline at pharmacological doses; >1.5x at nephrotoxic doses
expected_findings.severity_threshold: >1.5x baseline, or any elevation with concurrent tubular pathology
expected_findings.rationale: Insensitive renal marker - tubular degeneration precedes functional biomarker changes.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Adverse when accompanied by albuminuria (glomerular damage) or concurrent tubular degeneration
expected_findings.rationale: PS-ASOs competitively inhibit megalin-mediated protein reabsorption in proximal tubules.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Adverse when accompanied by hemodynamic changes, bronchospasm, or anaphylactoid reactions
expected_findings.species_note: NHP-SPECIFIC finding. Monkey complement is uniquely sensitive to PS-ASOs. Rats, mice, dogs do not show significant activation.
expected_findings.rationale: PS-ASOs bind Factor H, displacing it from C3b surfaces and causing uncontrolled alternative pathway amplification.

expected_findings.typical_magnitude: 2-10x for TNFa, IL-6, MCP-1 at supratherapeutic doses
expected_findings.severity_threshold: Adverse when accompanied by clinical signs or hepatic/splenic pathology
expected_findings.species_note: Rodent-predominant. NHP and human responses are substantially muted.
expected_findings.rationale: TLR9-dependent (CpG motifs) and TLR9-independent activation of innate immune cells.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: >25% increase over concurrent controls, or accompanied by tubular degeneration or elevated BUN/creatinine
expected_findings.rationale: Mild bilateral kidney weight increase reflecting tubular epithelial hypertrophy from oligonucleotide accumulation.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: >20% increase over concurrent controls, or accompanied by hepatocellular changes or ALT >5x
expected_findings.rationale: Drug accumulation in Kupffer cells, sinusoidal endothelial cells, and hepatocytes.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: >50% increase over concurrent controls, or accompanied by marked lymphoid hyperplasia (grade 4+)
expected_findings.rationale: Lymphoid hyperplasia from immune stimulation + macrophage hypertrophy from ASO accumulation in red pulp.
